title: Large-magnitude events unlikely in induced earthquake sequences
description: This study shows that earthquakes caused by fluid injection are often smaller than expected. Combining data and simulations, it links this to heterogeneous stress near wells and structural limits of fault networks.
content: We thank Yihe Huang, Julie Albaric, Stefan Baisch, Louis De Barros, Xiaowei Chen, Simone Cesca, Trenton Cladouhos, Kamel Drif, Paul Friberg, Peter Hennings, Won-Young Kim, Grzegorz Kwiatek, Xinglin L… [+460 chars]
de: Ereignisse großer Stärke sind in induzierten Erdbebensequenzen unwahrscheinlich. Diese Studie zeigt, dass Erdbeben, die durch Flüssigkeitseinspritzungen verursacht werden, oft kleiner sind als erwartet. Durch die Kombination von Daten und Simulationen wird dies mit heterogenen Spannungen in der Nähe von Bohrlöchern und strukturellen Grenzen von Verwerfungsnetzwerken in Verbindung gebracht.
en: Large-magnitude events unlikely in induced earthquake sequences. This study shows that earthquakes caused by fluid injection are often smaller than expected. Combining data and simulations, it links this to heterogeneous stress near wells and structural limits of fault networks.
la: Magnae magnitudinis eventus inconveniens est in sequentia terrae motus inductae. Hoc studium ostendit motus iniectio fluidorum causata saepe minor quam expectatio. Coniungens notitias et simulationes, hoc coniungit cum accentus heterogeneis prope puteos et structuras limites culpae ligula.
